title: CII - resolve no_leaked_credentials
author: sparerd
body_snippet:
This is to track resolution of CII requirement `no_leaked_credentials`
> The public repositories MUST NOT leak a valid private credential (e.g., a working password or private key) that is intended to limit public access. 

This requirement is broken because we use a default password for encryption unless a user provides a custom one. To resolve this, we must require a user defined password for encryption.

Possible related enhancements include:
- #187 
- #255

iterations.description: Issue title references 'CII - resolve no_leaked_credentials' which appears to be a CI/infrastructure badge requirement (CII Best Practices) but lacks details on what specific credential leaks exist or what changes are needed
Wontfix: Changing default encryption password is a breaking change for existing users with encrypted config files

comments.author: simonai1254
comments.snippet: Using encryption based on the Windows User instead of the generic key would also improve this a little...

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:

A beta build (v1.81.0-beta.4) including this fix is available from the fork's Releases page.

notes: No code fix exists. Default password mR3m is architectural - requires product decision on backward compatibility. Reclassified from testing to wontfix.
